sentence: Subjective:
The patient, Angela, presents with a history of recurrent kidney stones, with this being her seventh or eighth episode.
She reports that she has passed this current stone, which occurred approximately three days ago, and is no longer experiencing pain or blood in her urine.
She has a past medical history of hypertension and diabetes, for which she is currently taking 2.5mg of Norvasc and 500mg of metformin daily, respectively.
She reports that her blood pressure has been well-controlled at home, but her blood pressure in the office was elevated at 150/90.
She also reports a history of diet-related issues, including frequent consumption of fast food and sweets.
She denies any chest or abdominal pain, and reports that her symptoms of insomnia are managed with meditation apps.
Past medical history:
The patient has a history of hypertension and diabetes.
She has been taking 2.5mg of Norvasc daily for her hypertension and 500mg of metformin daily for her diabetes.
She has a history of recurrent kidney stones, with this being her seventh or eighth episode.
She also reports a history of diet-related issues, including frequent consumption of fast food and sweets.
Review of systems:
The patient denies any chest pain, abdominal pain, or other symptoms.
Current medications:
Norvasc 2.5mg daily for hypertension; Metformin 500mg daily for diabetes
Objective:
Vital signs:
The patient's blood pressure was 150/90.
Physical exam:
The patient's lungs are clear bilaterally, with no crackles heard on auscultation.
The patient has a grade 2 out of 6 systolic ejection murmur on cardiac exam.
The patient's abdomen is tender to palpation on the left lower quadrant, but this was not consistent with the patient's report of no pain.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has a history of recurrent kidney stones, with this being her seventh or eighth episode, which appears to be resolved at this time.
She also has a history of hypertension and diabetes, which are currently being managed with medication.
Problem list:
Kidney stones: resolved; Hypertension: active; Diabetes: active; Insomnia: suspected
Plan:
I prescribed Percocet as needed for pain management during her previous hospitalization for a kidney stone.
I will give the patient a referral to urology to evaluate her recurrent kidney stones.
I will also order a urinalysis and urine culture to ensure that the stone has been fully cleared.
I will continue the patient on 2.5mg of Norvasc daily for hypertension and 500mg of metformin daily for diabetes.
I will give the patient a consult to nutrition to help her manage her diet-related issues.
I will also prescribe 10mg of melatonin as needed for insomnia.
I will ask the patient to take her blood sugars before every meal to monitor her blood sugar levels.
Follow-up:
The patient will return in three months for a follow-up appointment.
